steps.title: Subtract to find the sum of the other three resistors
steps.content:
The 5 resistors have a combined resistance of 90 ohms. Two of these resistors sum to 60 ohms. The remaining three resistors must sum to:
$$90 - 60 = 30 \text{ ohms}$$

steps.title: Apply positivity constraints to determine the range for x
steps.content:
Let $x$ be the resistance of one of the other 3 resistors, and let the other two have resistances $r$ and $s$. Then:
$$x + r + s = 30$$
Since each resistor must have positive resistance:
  • $x > 0$ (the resistor itself must be positive)
  • $r > 0$ and $s > 0$ (the other two must be positive)
Since $r + s = 30 - x$ and we need $r + s > 0$, we must have $30 - x > 0$, which means $x \lt 30$.
Therefore: $0 \lt x \lt 30$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
